record_identity.title: Provisional Interaction and Pre-Invocation Tool-Arming Constraint

summary: This patch records the Caelestis corpus repair to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.4.1 responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0001. The repair splits §7.4.1 into weak-trigger interpretation, provisional interaction / accidental submission handling, and pre-invocation tool-arming constraints. It clarifies that lexical mention of executable modalities, upload-without-instruction events, accidental submission, partial prompts, staged uploads, draft prompt construction, or incomplete multimodal assembly do not by themselves authorise tool invocation, tool arming, model routing, renderer preparation, modality-specific UI activation, or execution-path reservation.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: PATCH-0002 remains the historical proportional tool-invocation repair and is not invalidated. Later recurrence telemetry showed a narrower execution-boundary gap: systems may enter or prepare execution pathways before formal invocation. This follow-on repair extends the repair surface from formal invocation to pre-invocation execution preparation, including premature image-generator arming, UI/modal workflow activation, renderer preparation, model or runtime routing changes, and modality-specific interface activation based on weak lexical cues or incomplete interaction states.
evidence_confidence: verified for VIGIL linkage; implementation verification pending exact Caelestis commit or PR reference

source_records.source_context: The maintainer reports that CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.4.1 was split into §7.4.1 Weak Trigger Interpretation Constraint, §7.4.1.1 Provisional Interaction and Accidental Submission Constraint, §7.4.1.2 Pre-Invocation Tool Arming and Execution-Path Preparation Constraint, and §7.4.1.3 Tool Classifier Handoff Signal Recognition. Existing §7.4.2 External Instruction Influence Check was preserved without substantive alteration. Tool Classifier Handoff Signal Recognition was renumbered from §7.4.1.1 to §7.4.1.3. Exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL was not supplied in this VIGIL task.

changed_files_or_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
change_details.change_summary: Amendment version 3.6 split §7.4.1 into separate controls for weak-trigger interpretation, provisional interaction and accidental submission handling, and pre-invocation tool arming / execution-path preparation. Tool Classifier Handoff Signal Recognition was renumbered from §7.4.1.1 to §7.4.1.3, and existing §7.4.2 External Instruction Influence Check was preserved without substantive alteration.
change_details.implementation_notes: The repair clarifies that lexical mention of executable modalities, upload-without-instruction events, accidental submission, partial prompts, staged uploads, draft prompt construction, or incomplete multimodal assembly do not by themselves authorise tool invocation, tool arming, model routing, renderer preparation, modality-specific UI activation, or execution-path reservation. PATCH-0002 is not invalidated; this is a follow-on repair extending the repair surface from formal invocation to pre-invocation execution preparation.
change_details.provisional_state_constraint: Provisional interaction states may support contextual interpretation, but must not independently authorise generation, editing, rendering, retrieval, mutation, publication, model-routing change, modality lock, or tool-mediated execution.
change_details.tool_classifier_handoff_constraint: Tool-classifier handoff signals do not retroactively establish execution intent or user consent where the interaction state was weak-triggered, provisional, accidental, or unresolved.
non_goals: Does not create a new failure mode.
Does not create a new observation record.
Does not update or overwrite VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002.
Does not make FM-0023 the primary failure mode.
Does not broaden this into a general image-generation safety patch.
Does not modify the VIGIL schema.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Prevent weak lexical cues, provisional interaction states, accidental submissions, or incomplete multimodal assembly from independently authorising tool arming, model or runtime routing change, renderer preparation, modality-specific UI activation, execution-path reservation, or tool-mediated execution. The repair preserves contextual interpretation while requiring explicit or sufficiently resolved execution intent before pre-invocation preparation proceeds.
impact_summary.known_limitations: Exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL is pending. Live runtime recurrence remains subject to monitoring. This patch does not itself verify vendor runtime behaviour.

Monitor FM-0001 for recurrence after the follow-on corpus repair.
monitoring_notes: PATCH-0002 remains valid as the earlier proportional tool-invocation repair.
PATCH-0013 addresses the narrower pre-invocation execution-preparation gap surfaced by recurrence telemetry.
Do not treat tool-classifier handoff signals as retroactive execution consent in weak-triggered, provisional, accidental, or unresolved interaction states.
